column1: 1 ^ Dominus illūminātiō mea et salūs mea~; ¿~quem timēbō~? Dominus prōtector vītae meae~; ¿~ā quō trepidābō~? ^ The Lord is my light and my salvation~; whom shall I fear~? The Lord is the protector of my life~; of whom shall I be afraid~?

column3: quī trībulant mē et inimīcī meī
column4: ipsī infirmātī sunt et cecidērunt. ^ Whilst the wicked draw near against me
column5: so as to eat my flesh
column6: they that trouble me and my enemies
column7: have been weakened and have fallen.

column2: nōn timēbit cor meum. Sī exsurgat adversum mē praelium
column3: in hōc egō spērābō. ^ If armies in camp should stand together against me
column4: my heart shall not fear. If a battle should rise up against me
column5: in this will I be confident.

column3: ut inhabitem in domū Dominī omnibus diēbus vītae meae~; ut videam voluptātem Dominī
column4: et vīsitem templum ejus. ^ One thing I have asked of the Lord
column6: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life~; that I may see the delight of the Lord

column1: 5 ^ Quoniam abscondit mē in tabernāculō suō~; in diē malōrum prōtexit mē in absconditō tabernāculī suī. ^ For he hath hidden me in his tabernacle~; in the day of evils he hath protected me in the secret place of his tabernacle.

column2: et nunc exaltāvit caput meum super inimīcōs meōs. Circumīvī
column3: et immolāvī in tabernāculō ejus hostiam vōciferātiōnis~; cantābō et psalmum dīcem Dominō. ^ He hath exalted me upon a rock
column4: and now he hath lifted up my head above my enemies. I have gone round
column5: and have offered up in his tabernacle a sacrifice of jubilation~; I will sing and I will recite a psalm to the Lord.

column4: quā clāmāvī ad tē~; miserēre meī
column8: with which I have cried to thee~; have mercy on me and hear me.

column1: 8 ^ Tibī dixit cor meum~; exquīsīvit tē faciēs mea. Faciem tuam
column3: requīrem. ^ My heart hath spoke to thee~; my face hath sought thee. Thy face

column1: 9 ^ Nē āvertās faciem tuam ā mē~; nē dēclīnēs in īrā ā servō tuō. Adjūtor meus estō~; nē dērelinquās mē
column3: Deus salūtāris meus. ^ Turn not away thy face from me~; decline not in thy wrath from thy servant. Be thou my helper~; forsake me not
column4: and do not thou despise me

column1: 10 ^ Quoniam pater meus et māter mea dērelīquērunt mē~; Dominus autem assumpsit mē. ^ For my father and my mother have left me~; but the Lord hath taken me up.

column4: et dīrige mē in sēmitam rectam propter inimīcōs meōs. ^ Set me
column7: and guide me in the right path

column1: 12 ^ Nē trādiderīs mē in animās trībulantium mē
column2: quoniam insurrexērunt in mē testēs inīquī
column3: et mentīta est inīquitās sibī. ^ Let me not be delivered over to the will of them that trouble me
column4: for unjust witnesses have risen up against me
column5: and iniquity hath lied to itself.

column1: 13 ^ Crēdō vidēre bona Dominī in terrā vīventium. ^ I believe to see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living.

column3: et sustinē Dominum. ^ Expect the Lord~; do manfully
column4: and let thy heart take courage
column5: and wait thou for the Lord.
